mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments requiring efficient orchestration of large language models, but may require careful resource management to avoid over-provisioning.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command line interface which is necessary for managing deployments.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.5.1/llm-d-linux-amd64

chmod +x llm-d-linux-amd64

sudo mv llm-d-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

```

> Download the llm-d CLI binary for Linux.
> Make the downloaded binary executable.
> Move the binary to a directory in your PATH.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Create a GKE cluster with 3 nodes in the specified region.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubectl to use the new cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Fetch the credentials for the created cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes in the cluster to verify their status.

steps.title: Install llm-d Helm Chart
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d application using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/

helm install llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d --create-namespace

```

> Add the llm-d Helm repository.
> Install llm-d in a dedicated namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for llm-d.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaling.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Apply autoscaling configuration.
> Apply monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes llm-d from your cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources, including any persistent volumes.

```bash

helm uninstall llm-d --namespace llm-d

kubectl delete namespace llm-d

```

> Uninstall the llm-d Helm release.
> Delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your current configuration and data. This step will upgrade llm-d to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

helm upgrade llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.5.1 --namespace llm-d

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> Backup current llm-d configuration.
> Upgrade llm-d to the specified version.
> Verify that the llm-d pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get all -n llm-d -o yaml > llm-d-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error creating load balancer'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient permissions or quota limits in your cloud provider.

**Fix:**
Check your cloud provider's IAM permissions and resource quotas.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified container image cannot be pulled, possibly due to incorrect image name or tag.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and tag in your deployment configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the application inside the pod fails to start due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough CPU resources available for the requested pods.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster or reduce resource requests in your deployment.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for dynamic multi-workload autoscaling and improved inference scheduling algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n llm-d' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
